title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: Between life and death there is a library, and within that library, the shelves go on forever. Every book provides a chance to try another life you could have lived. To see how things would be if you had made other choices.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship. Even bad code can function, but if code isn't clean, it can bring a development organization to its knees. This book is a must-read for any developer.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: A landmark volume in science writing by one of the great minds of our time, Stephen Hawking's book explores such profound questions as: How did the universe begin—and what made its start possible?

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A novel about a young Andalusian shepherd in his journey to the pyramids of Egypt, after having a recurring dream of finding a treasure there. A timeless classic about following your dreams.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt & David Thomas
description: Your journey to mastery. Straight from the trenches, The Pragmatic Programmer cuts through the increasing specialization and technicalities of modern software development.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: Cosmos is one of the bestselling science books of all time. In clear-eyed prose, Sagan reveals a jewel-like blue world inhabited by a life form that is just beginning to discover its own identity.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: The unforgettable novel of a childhood in a sleepy Southern town and the crisis of conscience that rocked it. A gripping, heart-wrenching, and wholly remarkable tale of coming-of-age.

title: JavaScript: The Good Parts
author: Douglas Crockford
description: Most programming languages contain good and bad parts, but JavaScript has more than its share of the bad, having been developed and released in a hurry before it could be refined.

title: The Origin of Species
author: Charles Darwin
description: Darwin's landmark work on evolutionary theory introduced the scientific theory that populations evolve over the course of generations through a process of natural selection.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian social science fiction novel and cautionary tale. Set in a totalitarian society where critical thought is suppressed, it follows Winston Smith, a low-ranking member of 'the Party'.

title: You Don't Know JS
author: Kyle Simpson
description: A deep dive into the core mechanisms of the JavaScript language. No matter how much experience you have with JavaScript, odds are you don't fully understand the language.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: The world-famous work of evolutionary biology, completely revised and updated, with two entirely new chapters and a new introduction by the author. A brilliant and controversial analysis of genetics.
